description: The NS200 has been the go-to naked sport bike for Indian riders for over a decade. It defined the segment and still holds its own against newer rivals. Raw, aggressive, and fun to ride — the NS200 is what happens when Bajaj builds a bike for enthusiasts.
cons: Engine gets loud and buzzy at high RPM
Rear seat is uncomfortable for pillion
Suspension is stiff for rough roads
Design is starting to show its age

common_problems.cause: Liquid-cooled engine struggles when air flow is minimal
common_problems.solution: Avoid idling for long periods. Use engine kill switch at signals.

common_problems.cause: Aggressive riding style puts more stress on drivetrain
common_problems.solution: Lube chain every 500 km. Check tension and alignment regularly.

common_problems.problem: Brake pad wear on front disc
common_problems.cause: Heavy reliance on front brake in city riding
common_problems.solution: Check brake pads every 3,000 km. Use both brakes evenly.

buying_advice.oem_vs_aftermarket: OEM accessories fit perfectly but cost more. For the Pulsar NS200, aftermarket offers good value for non-critical items like phone mounts and bike covers. For crash protection, go OEM or from a trusted brand.
money_saving_tips: Buy during festive season sales (Diwali, Republic Day) for 15-30% discounts
Start with essentials, add accessories as you identify actual needs
Check Amazon, Flipkart, and local dealers — prices vary significantly
common_mistakes: Buying a cheap helmet to save money
Skipping crash guard — one tip-over can cost thousands in repairs
Using WD-40 as chain lube — it is a cleaner, not a lubricant
Not reading the owner's manual for service intervals
